Container.Name: mem0-aio
Container.Overview:
Mem0 OpenMemory gives LLM apps a persistent memory layer with a web UI, MCP/API server, and pluggable vector backends.
[b]All-In-One Unraid Edition[/b] `mem0-aio` packages the OpenMemory UI, API, and embedded Qdrant into one Unraid-first container so beginners can get a working first boot without wiring a separate vector database.
[b]Quick Install (Beginners)[/b] 1. Install the template and leave the default appdata path in place. 2. If you want OpenAI immediately, set [code]OPENAI_API_KEY[/code]. 3. If you prefer local or alternate providers, you can leave the key blank, start the container, and then configure Ollama or another provider from the UI. 4. Open the Web UI, finish provider configuration, and optionally expose the direct API port only if you need external MCP clients.
[b]Power Users (Advanced View)[/b] - Advanced View exposes the practical upstream OpenMemory self-hosted environment surface plus AIO defaults for the bundled SQLite + Qdrant path. - You can keep the internal defaults, or point OpenMemory at Ollama, Anthropic, Groq, Together, DeepSeek, Azure OpenAI, Bedrock-compatible providers, and external vector backends such as pgvector, Weaviate, Redis, Milvus, Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Chroma, or FAISS. - Leave defaults in place for the easiest install. Only set the overrides you actually need.
[b]Important Notes[/b] - This wrapper is meant to simplify first boot on Unraid, not remove the real complexity of model/provider credentials and external-service tuning. - Actual memory generation still requires a valid LLM/embedder configuration. Leaving [code]OPENAI_API_KEY[/code] blank only keeps first boot simple; it does not make the app fully usable until you configure a provider. - The embedded Qdrant service is intentionally bundled because that is the critical dependency for the beginner AIO path.
Container.Changes:
[b]Latest release[/b] - v1.0.9-aio.1 [b]Dependency Updates[/b] - Update non-major infrastructure updates by @renovate[bot] - Update node.js to v24 by @renovate[bot] - Pin ubuntu docker tag to 186072b by @renovate[bot] - Update docker/build-push-action action to v7 by @renovate[bot] - Update docker/setup-buildx-action action to v4 by @renovate[bot] - Update docker/setup-qemu-action action to v4 by @renovate[bot] - Update docker/metadata-action action to v6 by @renovate[bot] - Update docker/login-action action to v4 by @renovate[bot]
[b]Documentation[/b] - Add repository guidance by @JSONbored
[b]Features[/b] - Initial commit for Mem0 AIO Unraid template with s6-overlay by @JSONbored - Complete AIO docker build for mem0 openmemory with all s6 hooks and awesome-unraid xml by @JSONbored - Add git-cliff release workflow by @JSONbored
[b]Fixes[/b] - Tighten changelog spacing by @JSONbored - Make releases manual and gate heavy workflows by @JSONbored - Harden publish and changelog range by @JSONbored
[b]Maintenance[/b] - Standardize README, add FUNDING.yml, and clean up legacy files by @JSONbored - Prepare for clean submodule by @JSONbored - Standardize template by @JSONbored - Add template sync workflow by @JSONbored - Revert to verifiable bot identity for non-repudiation by @JSONbored
[b]Other Changes[/b] - Force rebuild to publish docker image by @JSONbored - Security & CI: Fix node24 deprecation and package write permissions by @JSONbored - Link Mem0 as recursive submodule and update CI to include submodules in build by @JSONbored - Adjust Dockerfile COPY paths to match actual OpenMemory submodule structure by @JSONbored - Harden mem0-aio and add smoke testing by @JSONbored - Harden mem0-aio workflows and upstream tracking by @JSONbored - Add Codex repo memory notes by @JSONbored - Add renovate.json by @renovate[bot] - Merge branch 'main' into codex/harden-mem0-aio by @JSONbored - Reduce smoke-test CI usage by @JSONbored - Standardize funding and security docs by @JSONbored - Add standard community templates by @JSONbored - Consolidate CI workflows by @JSONbored - Consolidate CI workflows by @JSONbored - Merge main into consolidate-ci-workflows by @JSONbored - Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into codex/reduce-smoke-ci by @JSONbored - Fix awesome-unraid sync for protected main by @JSONbored - Standardize upstream-aligned image tags by @JSONbored
[b]New Contributors[/b] - @JSONbored made their first contribution in [#21](https://github.com/JSONbored/mem0-aio/pull/21) - @renovate[bot] made their first contribution
Full changelog and release notes: [url=https://github.com/JSONbored/mem0-aio/releases]GitHub Releases[/url]
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: ai memory mcp llm openmemory qdrant ollama anthropic groq deepseek embeddings vector database
Container.Requires: OpenMemory still needs a valid LLM/embedder setup for actual memory generation. For the easiest first boot, keep the bundled storage defaults and add provider credentials only for the models you intend to use.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Main OpenMemory web interface port.

Config.Name: API / MCP Port
Config.Description: Direct API and MCP port. The UI works without exposing this externally, but external MCP clients may need it.

Config.Name: AppData - OpenMemory Storage
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database, embedded Qdrant data, and AIO state.

Config.Name: OpenAI API Key
Config.Description: Optional on first boot. Required if you want OpenAI as the default provider. If blank, the container still boots so you can configure Ollama or another provider from the UI.

Config.Name: Default User ID
Config.Description: Default user namespace used by the API and MCP server.

Config.Name: [UI] Browser API URL
Config.Description: Default same-origin proxy path for the web UI. Leave this alone unless you intentionally want the browser to call a different API URL directly.

Config.Name: [UI] Public User ID
Config.Description: Default user ID shown in the browser client. Usually keep this aligned with USER.

Config.Name: [LLM] Provider
Config.Description: LLM provider choice based on the current upstream OpenMemory UI provider list.

Config.Name: [LLM] Model
Config.Description: Optional model name override. Leave blank to let OpenMemory use its provider default.

Config.Name: [LLM] API Key
Config.Description: Optional provider-specific API key if you are not using OPENAI_API_KEY or if you want the LLM to use a different secret.

Config.Name: [LLM] Base URL
Config.Description: Optional custom API base URL for OpenAI-compatible or provider-specific endpoints.

Config.Name: [LLM] Ollama Base URL
Config.Description: Optional Ollama URL. Example: http://host.docker.internal:11434

Config.Name: [LLM] Ollama Host Override
Config.Description: Optional host override used by upstream Docker Ollama auto-detection. Example: host.docker.internal

Config.Name: [Embedder] Provider
Config.Description: Embedder provider choice based on the current upstream OpenMemory UI provider list.

Config.Name: [Embedder] Model
Config.Description: Optional embedder model override. Leave blank to let OpenMemory use its provider default.

Config.Name: [Embedder] API Key
Config.Description: Optional provider-specific embedder API key.

Config.Name: [Embedder] Base URL
Config.Description: Optional custom embedder API base URL.

Config.Name: [Storage] DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Advanced override for the API database connection. Leave the default SQLite path for normal AIO usage.

Config.Name: [Legacy] API_KEY Alias
Config.Description: Legacy upstream alias used by older OpenMemory examples that referenced env:API_KEY. Leave blank unless you intentionally depend on that older pattern.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Qdrant] Host
Config.Description: Default embedded Qdrant host. Leave unchanged for the bundled AIO path.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Qdrant] Port
Config.Description: Default embedded Qdrant port. Leave unchanged for the bundled AIO path.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Qdrant] Disable Telemetry
Config.Description: Controls Qdrant usage-statistics reporting for the bundled embedded vector store. Default is true so the AIO image stays privacy-first by default.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Chroma] Host
Config.Description: Optional external Chroma host. When set with CHROMA_PORT, OpenMemory will use Chroma instead of Qdrant.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Chroma] Port
Config.Description: Optional external Chroma port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Weaviate] Cluster URL
Config.Description: Optional full Weaviate cluster URL. If set, it takes precedence over WEAVIATE_HOST and WEAVIATE_PORT.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Weaviate] Host
Config.Description: Optional external Weaviate host.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Weaviate] Port
Config.Description: Optional external Weaviate port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Redis] URL
Config.Description: Optional Redis-backed vector-store URL. Example: redis://:password@host:6379/0

Config.Name: [Vector Store:PGVector] Host
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL/pgvector host.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:PGVector] Port
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL/pgvector port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:PGVector] Database
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL database name for pgvector.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:PGVector] User
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL username for pgvector.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:PGVector] Password
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL password for pgvector.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Milvus] Host
Config.Description: Optional external Milvus host.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Milvus] Port
Config.Description: Optional external Milvus port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Milvus] Token
Config.Description: Optional Milvus token for authenticated deployments.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Milvus] Database Name
Config.Description: Optional Milvus database name.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Elasticsearch] Host
Config.Description: Optional external Elasticsearch host.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Elasticsearch] Port
Config.Description: Optional external Elasticsearch port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Elasticsearch] User
Config.Description: Optional Elasticsearch username.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:Elasticsearch] Password
Config.Description: Optional Elasticsearch password.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:OpenSearch] Host
Config.Description: Optional external OpenSearch host.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:OpenSearch] Port
Config.Description: Optional external OpenSearch port.

Config.Name: [Vector Store:FAISS] Path
Config.Description: Optional FAISS storage path inside the container or a mounted host path.

Config.Name: [Backup Export] User ID Filter
Config.Description: Optional one-shot filter for the bundled upstream export helper script. Only set this if you intentionally run the export tooling inside the container.

Config.Name: [Backup Export] App ID Filter
Config.Description: Optional one-shot filter for the bundled upstream export helper script.

Config.Name: [Backup Export] From Date (epoch)
Config.Description: Optional one-shot export filter. Epoch timestamp in seconds.

Config.Name: [Backup Export] To Date (epoch)
Config.Description: Optional one-shot export filter. Epoch timestamp in seconds.
